reviews.review_msg: assignments (essay & presentation) had no instruction. you had to wing it and then hope you got a good mark for it. exam questions were genuinely given beforehand so you knew exactly what the format was but it made marking a bit stricter. no slides, honestly this course felt like an excuse for him to talk about books he liked but it was pretty fun.
Class was ok read 2/6 books and ended with a B+, as long as you go to class you're fine. Sometimes he's vague when describing assignments, but his lectures are really funny to me icl. Chill class
Great professor overall with amazing lectures

Great prof overall, would be happy to take him again - Course had a lot of reading material, but comfortably enough time was given to complete them - Lectures were informative, engaging, and sometimes somewhat funny (jokes)

He makes lectures really interesting but it's easy to get lost if you aren't paying attention. Questions for final are genuinely posted out beforehand making life so much easier. Be warned that vocab matching is VERY subjective. Course-wise, be friends with your TA. They are the one marking all your work.

JD had interesting lectures, and it was nice to go and listen to him. However, he is very vague on what the assignments would be graded on, so it's really a guessing game. The final is really easy, and all the questions were given beforehand (you literally can't fail unless you misunderstand the questions). Not many assignments either.

Do you want a prof you can never please? Who gets upset if you use classroom material in your essay? Well look no further than JD Fleming. No matter how much effort or thought you put into your work it will never be enough. Feedback? Vague Grading? Inconsistent. If you want a class where you walk away confused or frustrated this is the one for you

Fleming is really knowledgeable professor and you can see his passion come out in his lecture style. No slides posted and the readings were pretty dry, but if you show up and take detailed notes you will do fine on the papers and the final exam! Super patient guy and was willing to take extra time when the class was struggling with certain topics.

Best professor I've ever had. I find it difficult to pay attention in other lectures, but this prof is so captivating and makes it easy for you to pay attention and engage. He makes all of the material easy to learn, and cares about his students. I highly recommend taking a class with him, you won't regret it.

Lectures can be a bit rambling but once he finds his rhythm they can be really amazing. Funny and seems kind enough. He places an emphasis on creating unique ideas and pieces of writing. Definitely have to work do well, but everything is done fairly.
